Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Agent identifies Zara Kovacevic's identity and retrieves contact information using contacts_search_contacts(query: "Zara Kovacevic"). Discovery: zara.kovacevic@moveops.com, Customer Support Specialist, User ID moveops_zara_kovacevic. Agent also looks up key contacts needed throughout the investigation: Liam Park via contacts_search_contacts(query: "Liam Park") - discovery: liam.park@stormcloud.io, CFO, StormCloud Analytics. Mina Hashimoto via contacts_search_contacts(query: "Mina Hashimoto") - discovery: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, Account Manager. May also look up Omar Ibrahim (omar.ibrahim@moveops.com), Suki Patel (suki.patel@moveops.com), Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com), and David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) as the investigation broadens.
OE 2: Agent investigates the StormCloud Analytics escalation. Searches email using search_emails(query: "StormCloud") or search_emails(query: "Liam Park") or search_emails(query: "Mina Hashimoto") to find the escalation context. Discovery: Liam Park's email (email_id: email_email_7b0cc9a6d376, subject: "Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review", sent from liam.park@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). Liam raises four issues: (1) Jae-won Kim's Austin apartment lease was never finalized with UrbanNest, still in temporary housing that expires in five days with no signed lease packet, (2) a promised goodwill credit does not appear on the invoice, (3) Simone Richter's desk reimbursement still in limbo, (4) Anya Petrova's warm referral to Ridgeline Robotics went unanswered. Agent also discovers Mina's reconstruction email (email_id: email_email_1a723752e25f, subject: "StormCloud escalation: reconstructing missed handoffs before I reply to Liam") showing the internal scramble referenced in the prompt. Searches Slack using conversations_search_messages(search_query: "StormCloud") and/or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Liam") for internal discussions.
OE 3: Agent investigates the Jae-won Kim lease issue in depth. Searches email using search_emails(query: "Jae-won Kim") or search_emails(query: "lease") or search_emails(query: "UrbanNest") to trace the timeline. Searches Airtable using airtable_search_records(base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim") to check the relocation record. Discovery: Record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin shows Status "In Progress" with Special Requirements noting the unsigned lease and UrbanNest guarantor paperwork holdout. Coordinator: Suki Patel. Origin: Seattle, WA to Austin, TX. Move dates: April 7-9 (already passed). Agent discovers that Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest flagged the guarantor packet issue on April 8, and Suki Patel replied on April 9 promising to send the paperwork by end of week but never followed through, identifying Suki as the person responsible for the lease failure.
OE 4: Agent searches Linear for StormCloud-related tickets to check for existing follow-up tracking. Uses linear_list_issues(query: "StormCloud") or linear_list_issues(query: "Jae-won") or linear_list_issues(query: "lease"). Discovery: Two critical Linear issues. (1) linear_issue_c97359216490 ("StormCloud Relocation - Jae-won Kim - SEA to AUS", priority 2), the main relocation ticket that was closed prematurely while the lease remained unsigned. (2) linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 ("Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents", ticket #412, priority 4/Low, created 2026-04-15, unassigned). The ticket description references Omar Ibrahim's support triage from email_email_1a3fb965161a and reveals a critical misjudgment: Omar checked the Airtable record, saw Status "Completed" and apartment "Confirmed", and the main relocation ticket was already closed, so he categorized it as a "low-priority document request" rather than an active housing exception. Labels: "Support, Low Priority, StormCloud." The agent connects Omar's misprioritization to the broader lease crisis. Also discovers linear_issue_a49b4d199139 ("StormCloud account incident reconstruction - Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop").
OE 5: Agent investigates the Ridgeline Robotics referral. Searches email using search_emails(query: "Ridgeline") or search_emails(query: "Kenji Watanabe") or search_emails(query: "referral"). Searches Slack using conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Ridgeline") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Anya"). Searches CRM using crm_search_deals(dealname: "Ridgeline") or crm_search_companies(name: "Ridgeline"). Discovery: A CRM deal exists: crm_deal_a95b4721982a ("Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)", $8,750, stage "Qualified To Buy", created 2026-04-24). The deal description reveals that Kenji Watanabe's initial outreach went unanswered for several days before recovery outreach on April 24. The referral was dropped initially (Anya Petrova at StormCloud provided the warm intro) but belatedly recovered as part of the crisis response.
OE 6: Agent investigates the $500 goodwill credit misapplication. Searches email using search_emails(query: "goodwill credit") or search_emails(query: "credit StormCloud") to find Julian Brooks' approval email (email_id: email_email_6542aa8a4c89) and Marcus Thorne's reply (email_id: email_email_ed395722f1d4). Discovery: Marcus confirms the accounting error, the $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, which is why Liam never saw the credit on StormCloud's actual bill. May also search Slack using conversations_search_messages(search_query: "goodwill") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "credit") for additional context.
OE 7: Agent investigates Simone Richter's stuck stipend reimbursement (the 4th issue from Liam's email). Searches email using search_emails(query: "Simone Richter") or search_emails(query: "stipend") or search_emails(query: "reimbursement"). Searches Slack using conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Simone") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "stipend"). Discovery: Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement was caught in Marcus Thorne's blanket freeze on stipend processing (email_id: email_email_087940af4103). The freeze was triggered by the expense auto-categorizer pilot issues and the TechComfort Supply duplicate receipt investigation. It blocked ALL pending reimbursements including valid claims like Simone's. Simone's stipend issue is discoverable through email (simone.richter@stormcloud.io) and Slack; no Airtable stipend record exists for this claim. Note: an agent that only investigates the top 3 issues from Liam's email and skips the 4th (Simone) will miss this entirely.
OE 8: Agent logs a CRM engagement note on the StormCloud Analytics account. Uses crm_search_companies(name: "StormCloud") to retrieve the company_id, then crm_create_engagement(engagement_type: "note", body: summary of the support review findings covering Jae-won Kim's unsigned lease and the guarantor packet failure, Omar's misprioritization, the dropped-then-recovered Ridgeline referral, the $500 goodwill credit misapplied to EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319, and Simone Richter's stuck stipend from Marcus's blanket freeze, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]). This addresses the prompt's request to "Log what I find as an engagement note on their CRM record so the account managers can see what support found."
OE 9: Agent checks and potentially updates Airtable relocation records. Uses airtable_search_records(base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim") to verify the record matches reality. The Jae-won Kim record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) shows Status "In Progress" with notes about the unsigned lease. Whether additional updates are needed depends on what the agent determines. This addresses the prompt's request to "If any relocation records for affected employees don't match what actually happened, update them so nobody is working off old information."
OE 10: Agent creates Linear follow-up ticket(s) for unresolved items. First checks for existing tickets using linear_list_issues(query: "StormCloud") (from OE 4). Noting that linear_issue_a49b4d199139 (reconstruction ticket) already exists, the agent creates a new issue using linear_create_issue(team: "team_operations", title: "Support follow-up: StormCloud unresolved items" or similar, description: documenting the unresolved items requiring action). The agent may also update Omar's ticket priority (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0) from Low to higher given the discovery that it was actually an urgent housing issue. This addresses the prompt's request to "For anything that doesn't already have a follow-up ticket, create one so nothing gets lost."
OE 11: Agent sends an email to Liam Park acknowledging the escalation. Uses send_email(sender: "zara.kovacevic@moveops.com", recipients: ["liam.park@stormcloud.io"], subject: "Regarding your recent escalation" or similar, content: brief acknowledgment that the support team is aware of the situation and reviewing each item, without making specific commitments). The prompt says "Keep it general though since I don't have full answers yet and this is probably something Mina or the account team should own the detailed response on." This email should be cautious and non-committal, consistent with Zara's T1 support role.
OE 12: Agent sends Mina the full breakdown of findings. Uses send_email(sender: "zara.kovacevic@moveops.com", recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], subject: "StormCloud support-side findings" or similar, content: comprehensive summary of every issue, current status, and what still needs to happen). Alternative: agent may send a Slack direct message to Mina using conversations_add_message to her DM channel. Either method is valid. The summary should cover: (1) Jae-won Kim's unsigned lease with UrbanNest guarantor paperwork never sent by Suki Patel, temporary housing expiring in 5 days, (2) Omar's misprioritized ticket (#412) categorized as low-priority because Airtable showed "Completed," (3) the $500 goodwill credit misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, (4) Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement stuck in Marcus Thorne's blanket stipend freeze, (5) the Ridgeline Robotics referral that was dropped for several days before belated recovery on April 24. Also notes what actions were taken: CRM engagement logged, Airtable records checked, Linear ticket created, email sent to Liam. This addresses the prompt's request to "send Mina the full breakdown of what I found so she knows what we're dealing with on the support side before she responds to the CFO."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Agent identifies Zara Kovacevic's identity and retrieves contact information using contacts_search_contacts(query: "Zara Kovacevic"). Discovery: zara.kovacevic@moveops.com, Customer Support Specialist, User ID moveops_zara_kovacevic. Agent also looks up key contacts needed throughout the investigation: Liam Park via contacts_search_contacts(query: "Liam Park") - discovery: liam.park@stormcloud.io, CFO, StormCloud Analytics. Mina Hashimoto via contacts_search_contacts(query: "Mina Hashimoto") - discovery: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, Account Manager. May also look up Omar Ibrahim (omar.ibrahim@moveops.com), Suki Patel (suki.patel@moveops.com), Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com), and David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) as the investigation broadens.
OE 2: Agent investigates the StormCloud Analytics escalation. Searches email using search_emails(query: "StormCloud") or search_emails(query: "Liam Park") or search_emails(query: "Mina Hashimoto") to find the escalation context. Discovery: Liam Park's email (email_id: email_email_7b0cc9a6d376, subject: "Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review", sent from liam.park@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). Liam raises four issues: (1) Jae-won Kim's Austin apartment lease was never finalized with UrbanNest, still in temporary housing that expires in five days with no signed lease packet, (2) a promised goodwill credit does not appear on the invoice, (3) Simone Richter's desk reimbursement still in limbo, (4) Anya Petrova's warm referral to Ridgeline Robotics went unanswered. Agent also discovers Mina's reconstruction email (email_id: email_email_1a723752e25f, subject: "StormCloud escalation: reconstructing missed handoffs before I reply to Liam") showing the internal scramble referenced in the prompt. Searches Slack using conversations_search_messages(search_query: "StormCloud") and/or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Liam") for internal discussions.
OE 3: Agent investigates the Jae-won Kim lease issue in depth. Searches email using search_emails(query: "Jae-won Kim") or search_emails(query: "lease") or search_emails(query: "UrbanNest") to trace the timeline. Searches Airtable using airtable_search_records(base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim") to check the relocation record. Discovery: Record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin shows Status "In Progress" with Special Requirements noting the unsigned lease and UrbanNest guarantor paperwork holdout. Coordinator: Suki Patel. Origin: Seattle, WA to Austin, TX. Move dates: April 7-9 (already passed). Agent discovers that Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest flagged the guarantor packet issue on April 8, and Suki Patel replied on April 9 promising to send the paperwork by end of week but never followed through, identifying Suki as the person responsible for the lease failure.
OE 4: Agent searches Linear for StormCloud-related tickets to check for existing follow-up tracking. Uses linear_list_issues(query: "StormCloud") or linear_list_issues(query: "Jae-won") or linear_list_issues(query: "lease"). Discovery: Two critical Linear issues. (1) linear_issue_c97359216490 ("StormCloud Relocation - Jae-won Kim - SEA to AUS", priority 2), the main relocation ticket that was closed prematurely while the lease remained unsigned. (2) linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 ("Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents", ticket #412, priority 4/Low, created 2026-04-15, unassigned). The ticket description references Omar Ibrahim's support triage from email_email_1a3fb965161a and reveals a critical misjudgment: Omar checked the Airtable record, saw Status "Completed" and apartment "Confirmed", and the main relocation ticket was already closed, so he categorized it as a "low-priority document request" rather than an active housing exception. Labels: "Support, Low Priority, StormCloud." The agent connects Omar's misprioritization to the broader lease crisis. Also discovers linear_issue_a49b4d199139 ("StormCloud account incident reconstruction - Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop").
OE 5: Agent investigates the Ridgeline Robotics referral. Searches email using search_emails(query: "Ridgeline") or search_emails(query: "Kenji Watanabe") or search_emails(query: "referral"). Searches Slack using conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Ridgeline") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Anya"). Discovery: A CRM deal exists: crm_deal_a95b4721982a ("Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)", $8,750, stage "Qualified To Buy", created 2026-04-24). The deal description reveals that Kenji Watanabe's initial outreach went unanswered for several days before recovery outreach on April 24. The referral was dropped initially (Anya Petrova at StormCloud provided the warm intro) but belatedly recovered as part of the crisis response.
OE 6: Agent investigates the $500 goodwill credit misapplication. Searches email using search_emails(query: "goodwill credit") or search_emails(query: "credit StormCloud") to find Julian Brooks' approval email (email_id: email_email_6542aa8a4c89) and Marcus Thorne's reply (email_id: email_email_ed395722f1d4). Discovery: Marcus confirms the accounting error, the $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, which is why Liam never saw the credit on StormCloud's actual bill. May also search Slack using conversations_search_messages(search_query: "goodwill") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "credit") for additional context.
OE 7: Agent investigates Simone Richter's stuck stipend reimbursement (the 4th issue from Liam's email). Searches email using search_emails(query: "Simone Richter") or search_emails(query: "stipend") or search_emails(query: "reimbursement"). Discovery: Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement was caught in Marcus Thorne's blanket freeze on stipend processing (email_id: email_email_087940af4103). The freeze was triggered by the expense auto-categorizer pilot issues and the TechComfort Supply duplicate receipt investigation. It blocked ALL pending reimbursements including valid claims like Simone's. Simone's stipend issue is discoverable through email (simone.richter@stormcloud.io) and Slack; no Airtable stipend record exists for this claim. Uses crm_search_companies(name: "StormCloud") to retrieve the company_id, then crm_create_engagement(engagement_type: "note", body: summary of the support review findings covering Jae-won Kim's unsigned lease and the guarantor packet failure, Omar's misprioritization, the dropped-then-recovered Ridgeline referral, the $500 goodwill credit misapplied to EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319, and Simone Richter's stuck stipend from Marcus's blanket freeze, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]). This addresses the prompt's request to "Log what I find as an engagement note on their CRM record so the account managers can see what support found."

OE 10: Agent creates Linear follow-up ticket(s) for unresolved items. First checks for existing tickets using linear_list_issues(query: "StormCloud") (from OE 4). Noting that linear_issue_a49b4d199139 (reconstruction ticket) already exists, the agent creates a new issue using linear_create_issue(team: "team_operations", title: "Support follow-up: StormCloud unresolved items" or similar, description: documenting the unresolved items requiring action). OE 11: Agent sends an email to Liam Park acknowledging the escalation. Uses send_email(sender: "zara.kovacevic@moveops.com", recipients: ["liam.park@stormcloud.io"], subject: "Regarding your recent escalation" or similar, content: brief acknowledgment that the support team is aware of the situation and reviewing each item, without making specific commitments). The prompt says "Keep it general though since I don't have full answers yet and this is probably something Mina or the account team should own the detailed response on." This email should be cautious and non-committal, consistent with Zara's T1 support role.
OE 12: Agent sends Mina the full breakdown of findings. Uses send_email(sender: "zara.kovacevic@moveops.com", recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], subject: "StormCloud support-side findings" or similar, content: comprehensive summary of every issue, current status, and what still needs to happen). Alternative: agent may send a Slack direct message to Mina using conversations_add_message to her DM channel. Either method is valid. The summary should cover: (1) Jae-won Kim's unsigned lease with UrbanNest guarantor paperwork never sent by Suki Patel, temporary housing expiring in 5 days, (2) Omar's misprioritized ticket (#412) categorized as low-priority because Airtable showed "Completed," (3) the $500 goodwill credit misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, (4) Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement stuck in Marcus Thorne's blanket stipend freeze, (5) the Ridgeline Robotics referral that was dropped for several days before belated recovery on April 24. Also notes what actions were taken: CRM engagement logged, Airtable records checked, Linear ticket created, email sent to Liam. This addresses the prompt's request to "send Mina the full breakdown of what I found so she knows what we're dealing with on the support side before she responds to the CFO."
